title: CommonSense Algorithm Manifest
description: Authoritative description of a publishable CommonSense algorithm bundle. The manifest is the only artifact the host inspects to decide whether and how to run an algorithm. Hash + signatures cover the canonical-JSON encoding of every field except `checksum` and `signatures`.

schemaVersion.description: Manifest schema version. Bumped on breaking changes; the host refuses unknown majors.

id.description: Globally unique identifier in the form `publisher-handle/algorithm-handle`. Kebab-case, lowercase, ASCII.

name.description: Human-readable title displayed in the catalog and run UI.

license.description: SPDX license expression for the algorithm source and bundled deps. Closed-source bundles use `LicenseRef-<id>`.

category.description: Catalog grouping for the consumer 'atlas of self-discovery' UI. Primary regions: appearance, senses, fuel, motion, wellness. Quality is calibration (QC). Legacy: trait (kept for older manifests). Future: ancestry, risk, research.

tier.description: Sandbox tier. v0 of CommonSense only loads `pyodide`.

module.description: Python module name resolvable inside the sandbox after the artifact is unpacked. Example: `prs.main`.

function.description: Callable inside `module`. Must accept the declared input and return the declared output.

entrypoint.description: Where execution begins. Resolved by the sandbox shim — the algorithm never imports host APIs directly.

mediaType.description: Tier-specific bundle media type. Locked per tier so the loader can reject mismatches immediately.

size.description: Bundle size in bytes. The host enforces this before download completes.

sha256.description: Lowercase hex digest of the on-disk artifact. Verified before unpack.

version.description: Exact interpreter release the bundle was built against. The host refuses runs against an incompatible engine.

dependencies.description: Fully pinned dependency closure. Every wheel/binary referenced by the bundle, with content hash. Reproducibility lives or dies here.

capabilities.description: What the algorithm is permitted to do. Default-deny: anything not listed here is unavailable inside the sandbox. The host enforces — these are not advisory.

network.description: Hardcoded to false in v0. Privacy invariant: plugin code has no network. Reserved as a property so future tiers can request scoped network for non-data hosts (e.g. reference panels) under explicit user consent.

fsRead.description: Read-only mount points the host exposes. Only `/input` is permitted in v0.

fsWrite.description: Write-only mount points. Only `/output` (a host-owned scratch dir) is permitted in v0.

progress.description: Whether the algorithm emits structured progress events via the host import. Rate-limited by the host regardless.

schemaRef.description: URI of a JSON Schema in this repo (e.g. `genomic-io.schema.json#/$defs/VariantSet`) describing what the host streams in. The host validates every record against this schema before it crosses the sandbox boundary.

referenceBuild.description: Reference build the algorithm was authored against. A mismatch with the user's data is the one wrong-answer mode no sandbox catches — surfaced loudly in the run UI.

requiredRsids.description: Every rsID the algorithm reads from the VariantSet. The host extracts exactly these from the user's retained genome and passes only them — so the algorithm sees what it needs without the rest of the genome crossing the sandbox boundary. Also drives the honest pre-run compatibility check (which of these the sample actually called). Declare ALL of them; the devkit cross-checks against the code.

schemaRef.description: URI of the result schema (e.g. `result.schema.json#/$defs/Result`). The host validates the algorithm's return value before showing it to the user.

supportedKinds.description: Which parsed input formats this algorithm can run against. Compatibility UI uses this to grey out cards that don't match the user's sample.

references.description: Evidence the rsID→trait association rests on. At least one is required: a test that can't cite its source is not publishable. The marketplace gate resolves every pubmed/doi citation and rejects any that don't exist (killing fabricated/AI-hallucinated references); a reviewer confirms relevance. Surfaced to users as 'Sources' on the result.

type.description: Citation kind. `pubmed`/`doi` are resolved for existence at the gate; `snpedia`/`clinvar`/`url` are checked for reachability.

id.description: PMID (digits, e.g. `21162744`), DOI (`10.xxxx/...`), SNPedia page (e.g. `Rs1815739`), ClinVar id, or a full https URL.

title.description: Optional human-readable label shown in the Sources list.

rsids.description: Optional: which of the declared requiredRsids this reference supports.

requirements.description: Resource budget the host will refuse to allocate. Profiled by the canonical gates at publish time; surfaced in the capability prompt.

keyId.description: Identifier of the ed25519 public key on the curator key list. The keyset is fetched from commonsc.io/catalog/keys.json and verified against the platform root.

sourceUrl.description: Public source repository if the algorithm is open. Required for the open-source library; optional for closed-source publishers.

checksum.description: SHA-256 over the canonical-JSON encoding of this manifest with `checksum` and `signatures` blanked. Detects post-publish tampering.

signatures.description: Both signatures must verify before the host will instantiate the artifact.

publisher.description: Publisher's ed25519 signature. Asserts authorship and version intent.

marketplace.description: Marketplace's ed25519 co-sign. Asserts the bundle cleared the canonical gates and human review.

keyId.description: Identifier resolving to a public key in the curator/publisher key list.

value.description: Base64-encoded detached signature over the canonical manifest bytes (checksum + signatures blanked).
